meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve Debugging (Case Study)
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome debugging blocks. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your coding journey.
content.answer_block: Debugging is the primary hurdle for student developers. By integrating Git version control with AI-driven diagnostics, you can isolate errors, revert broken logic, and generate precise fixes. Using Antigravity to map dependencies and Claude Code to refactor, you transform overwhelming errors into clear, actionable learning milestones for your portfolio.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a sea of red text. You have been staring at the same stack trace for three hours, and the deadline is looming. Every fix you attempt only births two new errors, and the fear that you simply aren't 'cut out' for this begins to creep in. This is the silent wall every developer hits, but it is not a sign of failure—it is a rite of passage.

Today, we break that wall. By combining the rigorous structure of Git with the analytical power of modern AI, you will stop guessing and start engineering. We are moving beyond trial-and-error, arming you with the workflows that senior engineers use to maintain massive codebases. Let's turn your debugging frustration into your most valuable skill.

steps.title: Establish a Clean Baseline
steps.description: Before debugging, you must ensure your current state is safely captured in Git so you can revert if the AI suggests a path that breaks functionality further.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and identify the most likely file causing the runtime error based on the stack trace provided.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'checkpoint: pre-debug attempt'

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Use AI to parse your error logs and map them to specific lines of code, preventing the common mistake of 'fixing' the wrong function.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Examine this stack trace and map the error to the specific function call in my codebase. Explain why this logic is failing.

steps.title: Simulate and Verify
steps.description: Create a minimal test case to ensure the AI's proposed solution actually addresses the root cause without side effects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a unit test that recreates this specific bug so I can verify the fix before applying it to the main codebase.

steps.title: Apply and Commit Atomic Fixes
steps.description: Apply the fix provided by Claude Code and commit it with a descriptive message to maintain a clean project history for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified function to handle the edge case causing the crash while maintaining current coding standards.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -am 'fix: resolved runtime crash in user authentication module'

steps.title: Document the Learning
steps.description: Don't just fix the code; explain the 'why' in your commit history. This turns a simple bug fix into a powerful interview talking point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the technical cause of this bug and the logic behind the solution for my project documentation.

pro_tips: Always commit before asking an AI to refactor; it prevents 'refactor fatigue' where you lose track of which change caused a regression.
Use 'git bisect' alongside AI to pinpoint the exact commit where a bug was introduced in larger projects.
Treat your Git history as a technical diary; future recruiters look at your commit messages to see how you solve problems.
When an AI gives you a fix, ask it to explain the 'why' behind the change, not just the 'what'. This is how you level up your skills.
Keep your AI prompts focused on one function at a time to prevent the model from hallucinating context outside the current file.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Now that you have mastered the Git-AI debugging loop, apply this workflow to an open-source project on GitHub to build your reputation as a reliable contributor.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical workflow to handle any bug that comes your way. Stop struggling in isolation and start building with a community that supports your growth. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
